JOINT NOTICE OF OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF ADRANIK KARAM IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STRIKE [CCP 425.16] FILED BY DEFENDANT CHEE, LYNDON Y. GEONETTA & FRUCHT LLP JACOBS, RAYMOND

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LYNDON CHEE, GEONETTA & FRUCHT LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 2, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: L. FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT RAYMOND JACOBS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. JACOBS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SATISFIED THE FIRST PRONG UNDER CCP 425.16, AS PLAINTIFF KARAM'S COMPLAINT ARISES FROM HIS WRITTEN OR ORAL STATEMENTS MADE BEFORE A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. SEE CCP 425.16(E)(1). MOREOVER, "EVERY COURT OF APPEAL THAT HAS ADDRESSED THE QUESTION HAS CONCLUDED THAT MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CAUSES OF ACTION FALL WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE." JARROW FORMULAS, INC. V. LAMARCHE, 31 CAL.4TH 728, 735 (2003). PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE ELEMENT OF PROBABLE CAUSE. SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED SHOW PRIMA FACIE FACTS THAT ALL REASONABLE LAWYERS WOULD AGREE THAT JACOBS' PREVIOUS CLAIM TOTALLY LACKED MERIT. JARROW, 31 CAL.4TH AT 743, FN. 13. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' LYNDON CHEE AND GEONETTA & FRUCHT SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THE FIRST PRONG UNDER CCP 425.16, AS PLAINTIFF KARAM'S COMPLAINT ARISES FROM DEFENDANTS' WRITTEN OR ORAL STATEMENTS MADE BEFORE A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. SEE CCP 425.16(E)(1). MOREOVER, "EVERY COURT OF APPEAL THAT HAS ADDRESSED THE QUESTION HAS CONCLUDED THAT MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CAUSES OF ACTION FALL WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE." JARROW FORMULAS, INC. V. LAMARCHE, 31 CAL.4TH 728, 735 (2003). PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE ELEMENT OF PROBABLE CAUSE. SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED SHOW PRIMA FACIE FACTS THAT ALL REASONABLE LAWYERS WOULD AGREE THAT DEFENDANT JACOBS' PREVIOUS CLAIM TOTALLY LACKED MERIT. JARROW, 31 CAL.4TH AT 743, FN. 13. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEXANDER HENSON, TEL. NO. (831) 659-4100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CASSIDY CHIVERS OF HINSHAW & CULBERTSON, TEL. NO. 362-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants' Lyndon Chee and Geonetta & Frucht special motion to strike is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants satisfied the first prong under CCP 425.16, as Plaintiff Karam's complaint arises from Defendants' written or oral statements made before a judicial proceeding. See CCP 425.16(e)(1). Moreover, "every Court of Appeal that has addressed the question has concluded that malicious prosecution causes of action fall within the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute." Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche, 31 Cal.4th 728, 735 (2003). Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the element of probable cause. Specifically, Plaintiff has failed show prima facie facts that all reasonable lawyers would agree that Defendant Jacobs' previous claim totally lacked merit. Jarrow, 31 Cal.4th at 743, fn. 13. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard Kramer, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Of Raymond Jacobs
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEXANDER HENSON, TEL. NO. (831) 659-4100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CASSIDY CHIVERS OF HINSHAW & CULBERTSON, TEL. NO. 362-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Raymond Jacobs' special motion to strike is granted. Jacobs' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant satisfied the first prong under CCP 425.16, as Plaintiff Karam's complaint arises from his written or oral statements made before a judicial proceeding. See CCP 425.16(e)(1). Moreover, "every Court of Appeal that has addressed the question has concluded that malicious prosecution causes of action fall within the purview of the anti-SLAPP statute." Jarrow Formulas, Inc. v. LaMarche, 31 Cal.4th 728, 735 (2003). Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the element of probable cause. Specifically, Plaintiff has failed show prima facie facts that all reasonable lawyers would agree that Jacobs' previous claim totally lacked merit. Jarrow, 31 Cal.4th at 743, fn. 13. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Richard Kramer, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/RAK)

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS LYNDON CHEE and GEONETTA & FRUCHT LLP'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS continued to June 4, 2013 to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 17, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
